_comment: CEO Orchestration — local settings for THIS repo (ceo-orchestration itself). Source of truth for distribution lives in templates/settings/ (settings.base.json + settings.stack.<name>.json). This file is a dogfooded BASE install — no tsc/vitest pre-commit gate because this repo has no TypeScript.
_squad_allowlist_comment: Sprint 11 CR2: empty default means nothing imports. Owners add entries via git; see ADR-039 + docs/squad-marketplace.md.
_sandbox_comment: PLAN-136 W3 S4b: OS-level Bash containment composed into the LIVE dogfood install (no longer SPOF fail-open — the env-layer sandbox sits UNDER the hook rail and does NOT fail open). Values copied from templates/settings/settings.stack.sandbox.json (PLAN-135 W2 / S4). enabled=true contains each Bash tool-call; allowUnixSockets keeps local IPC (audit filelock, git local sockets); filesystem.writablePaths scopes writes to the repo tree + scratch ($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, /tmp, /var/folders), HOME stays non-writable (startup-file class); network.defaultPosture=deny with allowedDomains = the PLAN-133 egress SoT (_lib/egress_taxonomy.py _PAIR_RAIL_HOST_SUFFIXES) + operationally-required hosts. autoAllowBashIfSandboxed SHIPPED FALSE (a fail-open hook + auto-allow makes allowedDomains the SOLE exfil control — deliberate adopter choice only).

sandbox._comment: OS-level containment for Bash tool-calls. SHIPPED enabled:false (Codex R1 P1) — the sandbox config is composed-and-ready in the live install, but NOT active until you flip enabled:true AFTER a sandbox probe (curl/gh/pip/npm/codex/anthropic) confirms allowedDomains covers your real workflows. When enabled, the harness runs each Bash command inside it; allowUnix keeps local IPC working (audit filelock, git local sockets); network.allowedDomains is the egress allowlist; default posture is deny.

filesystem._comment: Paths the sandboxed Bash process may read/write. repo = the project tree (so Edit/Write/build/test all work); /tmp = scratch (mktemp scratch dirs used by review_loop.py git-init, eval/probe scratch runs, hermetic venvs). HOME is NOT writable: keeps a sandboxed command from rewriting ~/.bashrc / ~/.claude profile/credential files (harvest D5 startup-file class). $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR expands to the repo root at install time.

network._comment: allowedDomains = PLAN-133 egress allowlist (see _network_allowlist_comment). Default posture is DENY: a host not on this list is unreachable from a sandboxed Bash command, which is the ~84%-fewer-prompts containment that retires the regex whack-a-mole (5 env(1) bypasses found in S223). deniedDomains is an explicit carve-back layer, empty by design.

sandbox._credentials_comment: PLAN-153 wave-backlog (Anthropic ADOPT sweep 2026-07) — Claude Code >=2.1.187 sandbox.credentials, schema verified against the 2.1.202 binary (files[].mode is literal 'deny'; envVars[].mode is 'deny'|'mask'; allowPlaintextInject optional, defaults false). Denies sandboxed Bash commands access to credential FILES (the subprocess-read class the permissions.deny Read() rail explicitly cannot cover — see _deny_baseline_comment) and unsets secret ENV VARS for sandboxed commands. INERT until sandbox.enabled=true (same flip discipline as the rest of this block). Honest limits: (1) this rail is HARNESS-side fail-open — the 2.1.202 loader drops an invalid entry or the whole block with a warning ('no credential protection is applied until it is fixed'), so the hook-side fail-CLOSED credential-leak guard in check_bash_safety.py remains the primary rail and this is defense-in-depth; (2) files paths resolve like sandbox.filesystem.* (absolute, ~ expanded, or relative to the project root) with NO glob support — the .env entries cover the repo ROOT only; subdir dotenv reads stay owned by the Read() deny rail + contamination CI; (3) envVars deliberately EXCLUDES GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY because gh, the `claude -p` eval harnesses, and the codex pair-rail all run through sandboxed Bash and may legitimately consume them — tighten only after an Owner-run probe (WATCH in docs/substrate-adopt-2026-07.md); (4) 'mask' mode (sentinel-in-sandbox + proxy inject on egress) is deliberately unused: it rests on egress-proxy TLS assumptions we have not probed; allowPlaintextInject stays explicit false.

PreToolUse._comment: CORE protocol enforcement: blocks named-agent spawns without ## SKILL CONTENT. Python hook via shim (resolves Python >= 3.9).

Bash safety: shlex-based parser replaces 5 substring-matching rules that false-positived on quoted strings during Sprint 2. Blocks: rm -rf (all flag combos), git reset --hard, git push --force/-f. Allows: --force-with-lease, quoted strings, compound commands with safe clauses. See check_bash_safety.py docstring for full logic.

PLAN-133 E1 (Goose-harvest) — adversary local-rules deny/ask gate. DETERMINISTIC local-rules scan over the Bash command, reading patterns from .claude/adversary.md (UNTRUSTED DATA, size-capped, read only INSIDE CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR). LOCAL-RULES-ONLY: NO model call on the hot path (the rite rejected sync Codex here — a measured 40x-600x p99 regression vs the ~5ms warm floor; real adversarial depth lives in the canonical/L3 Codex pair-rail). Default-OFF behaviorally: advisory (emit-only, ALLOW) until CEO_ADVERSARY=1 flips a deny/ask rule to BLOCK. Secret-in-command → fail-CLOSED (deny/flag, never transmit — E1 sends nothing, local-only). Fail-OPEN on any infra error. Own ADR (ADR-146) lands FIRST. Mirror in BOTH this template and .claude/settings.json or test_template_dogfood_parity reddens.

Plan lifecycle enforcement (Sprint 3 Item D + PLAN-019 P1-SEC-E): blocks illegal status transitions on .claude/plans/PLAN-NNN-slug.md files across Edit|Write|MultiEdit (P1-SEC-E closed a bypass where Write/MultiEdit let a sub-agent Write PLAN-NNN.md with status: done without required fields). Legal transitions per PLAN-SCHEMA.md §4. Out-of-scope edits (any path not matching PLAN-*.md) pass through.

Canonical-edit sentinel (Sprint 5 Phase 7 ADR-010 + PLAN-065 Layer A 2026-05-04): blocks Edit/Write/MultiEdit AND any mcp__* tool with write-shaped params (path/file_path/content/patch/diff/source/text) against canonical governance paths unless a matching Owner-signed sentinel exists. Layer A closes S81-tris gap: custom MCP tools (mcp__codex__apply_patch etc.) previously bypassed the gate entirely. See feedback custom_mcp_tools_governance_gap.md.

PROTOCOL.md semver cascade (PLAN-110 Wave D shipped v1.39.0 / wired v1.39.6 via PLAN-112-FOLLOWUP-plan-110-close S153 — closes false-closure F-1.1-1.1-7c108548 caught by S152 PLAN-112 audit). Fail-OPEN advisory: detects PROTOCOL.md edit without paired ADR-NNN-AMEND-M in same-session session_edits → emits protocol_edit_missing_amend_paired audit event + additionalContext WARN, never blocks. In-hook path filter targets PROTOCOL.md (matcher is broad per check_plan_edit.py convention). Position-in-chain: AFTER canonical-edit (sentinel-block fires first) + BEFORE skill-patch-sentinel (orthogonal scope). Doctrine port: spec-kit constitution.md:L302-L305 semver discipline.

Skill-patch sentinel (Sprint 11 Phase 4, ADR-031, CR1): blocks Edit/Write against .claude/skills/**/SKILL.md unless a paired GPG-signed SP-NNN.md proposal exists under .claude/proposals/ AND the pending commit carries the Skill-Patch-SHA trailer. Fail-closed by design (safety surface). CEO_SOTA_DISABLE does NOT disable this hook.

VETO-floor tier-policy sentinel (PLAN-043 Phase 4, ADR-064): blocks Edit/Write/MultiEdit against .claude/agents/code-reviewer.md or security-engineer.md whose diff modifies the model: field unless a dedicated Owner-signed sentinel with both 'Scope:' entry AND 'VETO-CHANGE:' marker exists. Third structural layer of VETO-floor defense (C-P0-3) in addition to _constants.VETO_HARDCODE module-load SHA256 assertion + apply.py VETO_HARDCODE_APPLY independent literal.

Arbitration-kernel HARD-DENY (PLAN-019 P1-SEC-A + PLAN-065 Layer A 2026-05-04): blocks Edit|Write|MultiEdit AND any mcp__* tool with write-shaped params on the hooks that enforce governance themselves + _lib policy/redact/audit-emit/contract primitives + .claude/policies. NO sentinel escape — only CEO_KERNEL_OVERRIDE=<reason-slug> + CEO_KERNEL_OVERRIDE_ACK=I-ACCEPT bypasses (bypass emits veto_triggered audit event with reason_code=kernel_override_used). Layer A closes S81-tris gap on kernel-protected paths.

Scratchpad scope gate (Sprint 11 Phase 7, ADR-034, M2): PreToolUse Bash hook that inspects python3 .claude/scripts/scratchpad.py invocations; blocks cross-plan --plan X when the active session derives Y. Allows non-scratchpad Bash and same-plan / no-plan invocations. Fail-open on infra error.

Cost/budget advisory (Sprint 11 Phase 6, ADR-033): PreToolUse Agent hook that tallies running tokens for the active plan and emits budget_exceeded audit event + systemMessage warning when CEO_MAX_PLAN_TOKENS is exceeded. ALWAYS allows in Sprint 11 (State 0 advisory). CEO_BUDGET_BYPASS=1 is the Owner-only escape hatch (rate-limited 10/24h per plan per H13). Sprint 12 flip criterion documented in ADR-033 §2 Flip Criteria Table.

PLAN-045 F-10-03 closure: wire the opt-in Read-injection scanner into the dogfood install. Advisory-only (always emits allow); fail-open on any infra error; per the hook docstring runs NFKC + zero-width strip + injection family scan and emits injection_flag audit event + systemMessage on hit. Kill-switch CEO_READ_INJECTION_SCAN=0 short-circuits with allow.

hooks.statusMessage: Scanning read content for injection patterns...

PreToolUse._comment: PLAN-075 v1.13.x narrow-promotion (ADR-106 + ADR-110): PreToolUse Pair-Rail Multi-LLM hook. When Edit|Write|MultiEdit targets an L3+ canonical-guarded path, invokes Codex MCP in read-only review mode and validates the response contains no write-shaped patches (apply_patch envelope, unified diff, JSON Patch RFC 6902). On Codex unavailable: fail-OPEN + breadcrumb. On write-shaped response: BLOCK + audit pair_rail_codex_violation. On clean review: ALLOW + audit pair_rail_review_passed. Sentinel-bypass short-circuits to ALLOW + audit pair_rail_sentinel_bypass. Kill-switches: CEO_PAIR_RAIL_DISABLE=1, CEO_PAIR_RAIL_TIMEOUT_S (default 30s).

PLAN-083 Wave 0a sub-agent 0.5: anti-CEO-overhead detector. PreToolUse hook detecting CEO doing work a sub-agent could do equally well (P1 sequential skill reads / P2 unrelated edits / P3 serial schema authoring / P4 grep/find serial / P5 cross-module test writes). Fail-OPEN on infra bug. Emit budget ≤20/day. Override CEO_OVERHEAD_ACK=1. Kill-switch CEO_ANTI_OVERHEAD=0. p95 latency ≤50ms (bench 0.029ms). PLAN-125 WS-1 (kooky-harvest): matcher widened to the broad tool set so this hook also writes the cheap per-tool-call PreToolUse lifecycle stamp (tool_use_id pairing record; emits NO audit-chain event per MF-SEC-5) co-located here to add NO new subprocess (MF-PERF-1). Lifecycle kill-switch CEO_TOOL_LIFECYCLE=0.

PLAN-102 Wave A (S142 2026-05-18) — cost-envelope HARD CAP gate on swarm-loop dispatch. Activates ONLY when BOTH CEO_SWARM=1 AND command body matches a real swarm coordinator signature (P1 #1 fold). Hook emits cost_envelope_capped on breach via emit_generic (P0 #2 fold; matches task_route_advised precedent). Default-OFF: fail-OPEN allow `{}` when CEO_SWARM unset/0. See ADR-133 §A + PLAN-102 §6b.

PLAN-125 WS-2 (kooky-harvest) — parallel-writer worktree-isolation enforcement. Default-OFF: INERT unless the writer self-sets CEO_PARALLEL_WRITER=1 (+ CEO_ASSIGNED_WORKTREE=<abs>). When ACTIVE it is UNCONDITIONAL + fail-CLOSED — DENIES any Bash/Edit/Write/MultiEdit whose RESOLVED target lands in the shared main checkout instead of the assigned dedicated worktree (ADR-049a AMEND-1; stops the S191 worktree-bleed). cwd resolved at the PreToolUse decision point (no TOCTOU via a later cd/git -C — MF-SEC-6); git refs/paths require a `--` end-of-options separator + allowlist regex (MF-SEC-7). Fail-OPEN only on an infra/parse bug of the hook itself. INFRA for non-conflicting parallel reads/reviews — NO throughput/speed claim (MF-4).

PLAN-124 WS-2 (ECC value-harvest) — adopter linter/formatter config protection. BLOCKING (maintainer ceremony). Allows first-time CREATION of a config (ENOENT) but BLOCKS any Edit/Write/MultiEdit that MODIFIES an EXISTING file in the UNAMBIGUOUS adopter-linter basename allowlist (eslint/prettier/biome/ruff/stylelint/markdownlint/shellcheck/tslint/flake8 — NOT pyproject.toml/setup.cfg/package.json, NOT governance configs which are the canonical guard's job per MF-L/debate K6). The insight: an agent 'fixes' a failing lint gate by relaxing the config instead of the code → 'fix the code, not the config'. Design choice = block-any-edit (ECC parity, revisitable; see module docstring). Escape hatches: CEO_CONFIG_PROTECTION=0 (session) or <repo>/.claude/.config-protection-disable (per-repo). User ceremony wires the ADVISORY variant (CEO_CONFIG_PROTECTION_ADVISORY=1 → allow + systemMessage steer, never blocks). Fail-OPEN on a hook bug; fail-CLOSED (bounded) only on a write tool with no resolvable file path. Raw tool input is never echoed (basename + resolved path only). MIT-attributed re-implementation of ECC scripts/hooks/config-protection.js.

PostToolUse._comment: Silent audit-log observer (Python): appends one JSONL line per Agent spawn to $HOME/.claude/projects/ceo-orchestration/audit-log.jsonl. Emits nothing on stdout (composes with reminder hook). Schema: .claude/plans/AUDIT-LOG-SCHEMA.md. Adds hook_duration_ms field + monthly rotation at 10MB.

Confidence gate (Sprint 9 Phase 1, ADR-019): PostToolUse Agent hook that invokes confidence_gate.py CLI over agent output, emits confidence_gate audit event, and ENFORCES blocking when CEO_CONFIDENCE_ENFORCE=1 (Owner-only flip; see ADR-019 + RELEASE.md). Advisory-only by default. CEO_CONFIDENCE_BYPASS=1 is the session-scoped escape hatch. Fail-open on timeout/infra per CLAUDE.md §Critical Rules. 10s timeout covers the CLI's 5s subprocess + overhead.

Output safety (Sprint 11 Phase 9, ADR-036, H14): PostToolUse Agent hook that scans tool_response for PII/secrets via mandatory pipeline (NFKC → zero-width strip → base64 decode ≤1 → Shannon entropy → regex). Advisory Sprint 11 (flag mode); emits output_safety_flag audit event, output preserved. CEO_OUTPUT_SAFETY_MODE=redact switches to [REDACTED:TYPE] substitution. CEO_SOTA_DISABLE=1 short-circuits to allow no-op. Sprint 12 flag→redact flip criterion in ADR-036.

Sub-agent fabrication detection (PLAN-059 Phase 0, ADR-080): scans Agent dispatch responses for the 4 fabrication formats observed in Sessions 61+62 rail anomaly (function_calls XML, tool_use JSON, tool_call JSON, **Tool Use markdown) + bonus tool_response fake. Default ADVISORY: emits veto_triggered audit event with reason_code=subagent_fabrication_detected. CEO_SUBAGENT_FABRICATION_BLOCK=1 escalates to systemMessage warning. CEO_SUBAGENT_FABRICATION_DEBUG=1 dumps full response to /tmp/h4-fabrication-<sha8>.json. Fail-open on any internal error. Activated via Owner-signed sentinel at .claude/plans/PLAN-059/architect/round-1/approved.md (GPG: 00000000).

hooks.statusMessage: Scanning sub-agent response for fabrication patterns...

PostToolUse._comment: After any Agent spawn, remind CEO to verify the agent respected the file assignment. [harness-noop-ok] — intentional constant-emitter advisory echo (PLAN-153 Wave E harness-config gate no-op annotation).

hooks.command: echo '{"decision":"allow","message":"POST-AGENT: Check git diff to verify file assignment compliance. Run: git diff --name-only"}'
hooks.statusMessage: Reminding CEO to verify file assignment...

PostToolUse._comment: PLAN-020 Phase 2 (ADR-051): PostToolUse observer for Read tool calls. When a sub-agent Reads a SKILL.md under .claude/skills/**, re-hash the content and emit a forensic breadcrumb to audit-log. Observer-only: always returns allow. Purpose: detect TOCTOU between spawn-time SHA-256 pin and actual Read (attacker swap mid-session). Fail-open on any error.

PLAN-029 / ADR-057: PostToolUse output-scan redaction. Scans tool responses for 3 families — unicode-injection (bidi/zero-width), telemetry strings (12 vendors), OWASP LLM Top 10 subset (LLM01/02/06/08/10). Advisory-only at State 0; advisory banner systemMessage on hit. Kill-switches: CEO_OUTPUT_SCAN=0 (master) + CEO_OUTPUT_SCAN_UNICODE / _TELEMETRY / _LLM10 (per-family).

PLAN-042 ITEM 7 (FINDING-17): SKILL.md bootstrap TOCTOU forensic observer. After any Edit|Write|MultiEdit on .claude/skills/**/SKILL.md, computes sha256 of resulting file and correlates with any recent skill_bootstrap_used audit event. Emits skill_bootstrap_post_hash with anomaly flag if delay > 5s (possible concurrent-race). Observer-only (advisory). Kill-switch: CEO_SKILL_BOOTSTRAP_POST=0.

PLAN-058 Phase A (ADR-077): PostToolUse scanner for WebFetch + WebSearch tool responses. Scans for prompt-injection (scan-injection.py 6 families) + harness-mimicry (_lib/injection_patterns.py 4 families). Advisory only; never blocks. Kill-switch CEO_WEBFETCH_INJECTION_SCAN=0. Incident 2026-04-24 remediation.

hooks.statusMessage: Scanning web content for injection patterns...

PostToolUse._comment: PLAN-052 / ADR-083: PostToolUse scanner for MCP tool responses. Scans for harness-mimicry + directive-prose patterns (4 families reused from _lib/injection_patterns.py via _lib/mcp_injection_scan.py wrapper). ADVISORY only — never blocks. Emits mcp_injection_finding audit event when match fires (gated on _lib/audit_emit.py kernel-override extension; degrades to advisory-only if extension not yet applied). Kill-switch CEO_MCP_SCANNER_DISABLE=1. Closes PLAN-058 C-P0-01 G4 (MCP tool result injection gate).

hooks.statusMessage: Scanning MCP response for injection patterns...

PostToolUse._comment: PLAN-081 Phase 1-full / R1 S-Sec-5: PostToolUse Codex MCP ingress sanitization. Scans Codex stdout for prompt-injection patterns (harness_mimicry [SYSTEM:, xml_system_tag <system>, tool_use_forgery <tool_use>) BEFORE Claude consumes the next turn. Emits pair_rail_codex_injection_detected audit event on match. ADVISORY only per ADR-106 (PostToolUse cannot block). Restricted to Codex MCP tool names via matcher; the broader mcp__.* scanner above (PLAN-052) covers other MCP servers' generic injection patterns. Fail-open: import / parse / scan failure → allow + breadcrumb.

hooks.statusMessage: Scanning Codex response for injection patterns...

PostToolUse._comment: PLAN-085 Wave E.4 — PostToolUse Bash canonical-edit forensic audit. Advisory only (never blocks); emits canonical_edit_completed breadcrumb when Bash command targets a canonical governance path via write-shape operators (> / >> / tee / sed -i / git checkout). Composition with E.3 PreToolUse interceptor: E.3 blocks at intent, E.4 records at completion.

PLAN-128 W1 #1+#4: after-edit verify dispatcher (advisory default-on; CEO_VERIFY_AFTER_EDIT=0 to disable). Runs syntax/lint on the changed file; surfaces findings as additionalContext for self-repair. Fail-OPEN.

PostToolUseFailure._comment: PLAN-125 WS-1 (kooky-harvest): per-tool-call lifecycle FAILURE coverage. Fires only on the rare tool-failure path (not the hot success path). Reuses check_output_secrets.py — it reads the native duration_ms + tool_use_id, pairs with the PreToolUse stamp, and emits tool_call_lifecycle_recorded with success=false (the PostToolUseFailure phase IS the success signal — no marker scan, MF-SEC). Routes through the dedicated scrub-branch + allowlist (NEVER _EMIT_GENERIC_PASSTHROUGH, MF-SEC-2); raw mcp__* tool name → mcp_other (MF-SEC-1); duration_bucket only, never raw ms (MF-SEC-3). Advisory + fail-open: a telemetry emit failure never blocks. Kill-switch CEO_TOOL_LIFECYCLE=0.

SessionStart._comment: PLAN-028 / ADR-056 lifecycle hook. Fires at session boot — audit init + Gate-1 cache warmup + governance health check. Fail-open: any error → allow + breadcrumb. Kill-switch: CEO_EXTENDED_LIFECYCLE=0.
PLAN-128 W2: turbo what's-on line + first-run banner + (opt-in CEO_AUTO_BOOT=1) /ceo-boot nudge. Advisory additionalContext; fail-open. Kill: CEO_TURBO=0 / .claude/turbo-off.

SessionEnd._comment: PLAN-028 / ADR-056 lifecycle hook. Fires at session close — audit closeout + memory persistence verify + audit-log filelock drain. Fail-open. Kill-switch: CEO_EXTENDED_LIFECYCLE=0.

UserPromptSubmit._comment: PLAN-028 / ADR-056 lifecycle hook. Fires pre-model-inference on each user prompt — redact-hit breadcrumb + 5-family injection smell-test + advisory banner on hit. Observer-only at State 0 (never blocks). Kill-switches: CEO_EXTENDED_LIFECYCLE=0 (disables hook), CEO_PROMPT_INJECTION_SCAN=0 (disables only the scan).

Stop._comment: PLAN-028 / ADR-056 lifecycle hook. Fires on SIGINT / SIGTERM / user_stop — emit session_stop event + filelock drain + stale-lock release. Graceful cleanup only; never blocks. Kill-switch: CEO_EXTENDED_LIFECYCLE=0.

PLAN-128 W1 #3: cross-model review detector (detect-only default; CEO_CODEX_USER_REVIEW_AUTO=1 to auto-run codex; CEO_CODEX_USER_REVIEW=0 to disable). On a NEW risky diff advises `codex review --uncommitted`. Fail-OPEN.

PLAN-128 W2: auto review-loop. OPT-IN (CEO_REVIEW_LOOP=1; default OFF/no-op). On a risky+unreviewed uncommitted diff, BLOCKS Stop with a 'run codex review --uncommitted' nudge, bounded at 3 iters then fail-open. READ-ONLY. Kill: CEO_REVIEW_LOOP=0.

PLAN-134 W1 #5 (E5-F2): closeout-guard — ADVISORY Stop reminder when the session did substantive work without a closeout (CLAUDE.md §Current Work + CHANGELOG + memory) and/or an Owner-GPG finish-*.sh ceremony is pending (staged/** or scripts/local/ newer than the last tag). Fail-OPEN, never blocks. Kill: CEO_CLOSEOUT_GUARD=0.

SubagentStop._comment: PLAN-045 P0-09 (b) — Artifact Paradox fluency-marker advisory. Scans sub-agent output for confidence phrases ('all done', 'tests green', 'perfect', etc). Adaptive threshold by length (2 markers <500ch, 4 <5000ch, 8 >=5000ch). On threshold-crossed: emits systemMessage advisory + fluency_nudge audit event. Observer-only; never blocks. Kill-switch: CEO_FLUENCY_NUDGE=0. See PROTOCOL.md §Artifact Paradox + pitfall LLM-001.

hooks.statusMessage: Checking for Artifact Paradox fluency markers...

PreCompact._comment: PLAN-135 W2 H1 (ADR-153): PreCompact continuity snapshot — before the transcript collapses (manual /compact or auto context-window threshold), snapshots plan-id + execution-unit position + ceremony flags to the plan scratchpad + reads the HMAC-chain anchor. ADVISORY, fail-OPEN, never blocks. Emits closed-enum compaction_continuity_snapshot. Kill: CEO_COMPACTION_CONTINUITY=0.

PostCompact._comment: PLAN-135 W2 H1 (ADR-153): PostCompact governance reinjection — after compaction, reads the PreCompact snapshot from the plan scratchpad and reinjects governance POINTERS (active PLAN, execution-unit position, Gate-1 re-read reminder, pending ceremonies) via additionalContext. POINTERS ONLY, never file contents (injection surface). ADVISORY, fail-OPEN. Emits closed-enum compaction_context_reinjected. Kill: CEO_COMPACTION_CONTINUITY=0.

ConfigChange._comment: PLAN-135 W2 H2: ConfigChange guard — ADVISORY audit + advisory-block of out-of-band settings.json tamper (the S197 class). Resolves multi-layer settings via _lib/effective_config (shared FORBIDDEN-KEYS source) + classifies tamper on the settings-FILE surfaces; emits config_change_observed (benign) / config_change_forbidden_key (advisory-block, ONE per tamper class). Fail-OPEN, never blocks on infra. Honest coverage boundary: disarmed by disableAllHooks + blind to outside-harness edits; compensators = /ceo-boot tripwires (S3) + W5 O10 OTEL witness (ADR-153 §H2). Kill: CEO_CONFIG_CHANGE_GUARD=0.

SubagentStart._comment: PLAN-135 W2 H3: SubagentStart lifecycle recorder — records the spawn instant + spawn context (agent_type, session binding) into a local sidecar keyed by sha256(agent_id)[:16] (raw agent_id NEVER persisted). The SubagentStop half (check_fluency_nudge.py) consumes it + agent_transcript_path to emit ONE closed-enum subagent_lifecycle_observed bracket (per-agent wall-time + token + claim; feeds persona-ledger). ADVISORY, fail-OPEN, never blocks, NO audit action from this hook. Kill: CEO_SUBAGENT_LIFECYCLE=0.

Setup._comment: PLAN-135 W2 H8: Setup-event post-install self-verification (init matcher) — validate-governance --fast + verify-counts + hook exec-bits (the S228 exec-bit class) + CLAUDE_ENV_FILE allowlist persistence (explicit CEO_* include-list; every override/escape-hatch/kill-switch EXCLUDED, S185/S197 stale-override class). ADVISORY + fail-open; NEVER blocks. Kill-switch: CEO_SETUP_VERIFICATION=0.

_enforce_available_models_comment: PLAN-153 wave-backlog (Anthropic ADOPT sweep 2026-07) — Claude Code >=2.1.202 verified semantics: when true and availableModels is a non-empty array, the DEFAULT model selection is also constrained to the allowlist (if the tier default is not in availableModels, Default resolves to the first allowed entry). Closes Default-model drift when the harness tier default moves ahead of our pinned set. Schema-verified optional boolean, valid in project settings (cascade-trust mode). Caveat straight from the binary: if a MANAGED-POLICY settings source exists but fails to load, the harness refuses cascade-trust mode and model enforcement from user/project settings is DISABLED with a warning — fail-open at harness level; acceptable because the availableModels pins still gate explicit selection.
_deny_baseline_comment: PLAN-153 Wave E item 3 — coarse harness-level deny baseline (docs/deny-baseline.md). Entries 8-27 of permissions.deny below mirror scripts/install.sh DENY_BASELINE_ENTRIES exactly: credential-store reads (~/.ssh, ~/.aws, npm/gcloud/kube/docker/git-credentials/netrc/pypirc), dotenv-family reads (SPECIFIC .env variants only — a deny rule cannot carry allow exceptions, so .env.example/.sample/.template stay readable by omission; unlisted variants like .env.secret pass this backstop), and the curl|bash tripwire. Honest limits: Read deny does not cover arbitrary subprocesses opening files, Bash(curl * | bash) is bypassable by rephrasing (that class is owned by check_bash_safety.py), and **/.env is cwd-anchored. Gate floor: check_harness_config.py DENY_BASELINE (the original 7 entries) must remain a subset of this list.
_skill_budget_comment: PLAN-135 W1 S7: skillListingBudgetFraction caps the skill-listing context tax (fraction of context window in chars; CLI default 0.01 = 8000 chars at a 200k window); skillOverrides name-only demotes 0-dispatch domain-tier skills. Regenerate: python3 .claude/scripts/skill-budget-generator.py --jq-fragment
